stories.headline: Live updates: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s multi-day wedding celebration is underway - CNN
stories.summary: An event believed to be the rehearsal dinner for the couple kicked off Swift and Kelce’s wedding festivities ahead of Friday’s big celebration, according to people familiar with the planning. Follow…

stories.headline: Madonna Finds a Nonstop Groove on Her Best Album in 20 Years - Rolling Stone
stories.summary: On 'Confessions II,' Madonna shows how dramatic and ecstatic a dance floor can be. Our review.

stories.headline: Blake Lively confirms major friendship fallout with Taylor Swift as she skips wedding celebration - Yahoo
stories.summary: Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds were spotted in Upstate New York on Thursday as rumors swirled about whether the couple received an invite to Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding celebration in NYC…

stories.headline: Britney Spears Keeps Low Profile During Holiday Weekend Outing - TMZ
stories.summary: Britney Spears kept things low-key ahead of the Fourth of July holiday weekend, stepping out for a drive in Los Angeles with her bodyguard.

stories.headline: ‘Young Washington’ Review: Generic Rah-Rah Biopic Gets Cut Down by Its Own Credits - TheWrap
stories.summary: Angel Studios sucks the good will out of yet another semi-competent production with their last-minute, sketchy panhandling.

stories.headline: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce make donations to KC's Harvesters food bank, Children's Mercy, other groups - KMBC
stories.summary: As the rumored wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce nears, the couple is giving back to communities around the country, including Kansas City.

stories.headline: Midjourney Seeks to Reveal Studios’ Use of AI in High-Stakes Copyright Battle - Variety
stories.summary: Midjourney is appealing a magistrate judge's order limiting its ability to reveal how studios are using artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: ‘Don’t Mess With a Comedic Rapper’: How Afroman Became a Free-Speech Hero - Rolling Stone
stories.summary: Afroman, the rapper famous for "Because I Got High," talks the police raid on his home and viral defamation trial that made him a First Amendment hero
